The nth term is the sum of the first n terms of the sequence of differences. 

The first term is 1. The second term is 1 + 10 = 11. The third term is 11 +14=25. The fourth term is 25 +18=43. 

So the general formula for the nth term is 1 + sum_{k=2}^n (4k + 2).

But the sum from k=2 to n of (4k +2) can be written as sum_{k=2}^n 4k + sum_{k=2}^n 2. 

Which is 4*sum_{k=2}^n k + 2*(n-1). 

Sum from k=2 to n of k is equal to sum from k=1 to n of k minus 1. Which is n(n+1)/2 - 1. 

So substituting:

4*(n(n+1)/2 -1) + 2(n-1) = 4*( (n(n+1)/2) -1 ) + 2(n-1) 

= 2n(n+1) -4 + 2n -2 

= 2n² + 2n -4 + 2n -2 

= 2n² +4n -6

In this case, the first term of the differences is 10 (for k=1), then 14 (k=2), etc. So the sum of the first (n-1) terms is (n-1)/2 * [2*10 + (n-2)*4].
